_comment: Per-chart narratives. CI (build_prompt.py -> GitHub Models -> write_narrative.py) overwrites these from live numbers; this committed copy is the fallback so the page always reads well. source = 'fallback' | 'github-models'.

notes.basket: The breakfast basket, an index averaging prices of coffee, sugar, wheat, and orange juice with a 2015 base of 100, currently stands at 141, reflecting a 41% increase since 2015. This index aggregates global commodity prices sourced from FRED and the IMF, illustrating a notable rise that has unfolded unevenly over time. Recent movement in these prices is not uniform across the components, indicating localized influences rather than broad-based inflation.
notes.movers: Among the basket's components, coffee prices have surged the most, up 99% since 2015, followed by orange juice at 34%, wheat at 19%, and sugar at 13%. These changes stem mainly from supply shocks related to weather and crop diseases—such as citrus greening and hurricanes impacting orange juice, and frosts and droughts affecting arabica coffee regions. The thin nature of soft commodity markets magnifies the price swings caused by such localized disruptions.
notes.why: The headline rise in breakfast commodity prices is primarily driven by weather-sensitive crops with constrained supplies. These shocks are specific and may not signal widespread inflation, but given the vulnerability to climatic events, cost pressures in these commodity inputs could persist for an extended period.
